title: America on the world stage
description: Test your skills on how and why American foreign policy developed and expanded over time.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad59f06ba586910a2d2a1dec1db3b13e.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ograbme.jpg), 1807

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Embargo Act
The Treaty of Ghent
The Missouri Compromise
The Monroe Doctrine

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad59f06ba586910a2d2a1dec1db3b13e.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ograbme.jpg), 1807

**The policy referenced in the image was most directly a response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: British impressment of American soldiers
French pirates docking in American ports
Spanish disputes over the Floridian border
German and Irish immigration to the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ad59f06ba586910a2d2a1dec1db3b13e.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ograbme.jpg), 1807

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Banned all American ships from sailing to foreign ports for trade
Prohibited new immigrants the right to vote
Limited European colonization in the Western Hemisphere
Regulated the number of goods merchants could send to the British and the French

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Opening up trade abroad became crucial for the Republican leaders. Desiring as they did the United States to remain predominantly rural and agricultural, they were confronted with the problem of ensuring sufficient markets for the agricultural surpluses of America’s many hardworking and productive farmers. Since the Southern Republicans did not want America to develop huge urban centers, they could not assume the existence of a large domestic market for the surpluses of farm goods. If the farmers were unable to sell their produce somewhere, they would stagnate, slip into mere subsistence farming, and become idle and lazy and eventually morally unfit for republican government.”

>-Source: Gordon S. Wood, *Empire of Liberty: A History of the Early Republic, 1789-1815,* 2009

**Which of the following statements best summarizes the author’s argument in the passage above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Democratic-Republicans wanted to increase foreign trade to continue to sell large amounts of agricultural goods
Democratic-Republicans wanted to decrease foreign trade as foreign competition undercut the price of manufactured goods
Democratic-Republicans encouraged farmers to diversify their agricultural products to limit their environmental impact
Democratic-Republicans increased tariffs to drive up the price of foreign agricultural products

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Opening up trade abroad became crucial for the Republican leaders. Desiring as they did the United States to remain predominantly rural and agricultural, they were confronted with the problem of ensuring sufficient markets for the agricultural surpluses of America’s many hardworking and productive farmers. Since the Southern Republicans did not want America to develop huge urban centers, they could not assume the existence of a large domestic market for the surpluses of farm goods. If the farmers were unable to sell their produce somewhere, they would stagnate, slip into mere subsistence farming, and become idle and lazy and eventually morally unfit for republican government.”

>-Source: Gordon S. Wood, *Empire of Liberty: A History of the Early Republic, 1789-1815,* 2009

**Which of the following pieces of historical evidence from the United States census would best support the argument in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Data showing changes in agricultural production and price
Data showing population growth in rural areas in the Northeast
Data showing the growth of railroad lines
Data showing changes in wages for workers in textile mills

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Opening up trade abroad became crucial for the Republican leaders. Desiring as they did the United States to remain predominantly rural and agricultural, they were confronted with the problem of ensuring sufficient markets for the agricultural surpluses of America’s many hardworking and productive farmers. Since the Southern Republicans did not want America to develop huge urban centers, they could not assume the existence of a large domestic market for the surpluses of farm goods. If the farmers were unable to sell their produce somewhere, they would stagnate, slip into mere subsistence farming, and become idle and lazy and eventually morally unfit for republican government.”

>-Source: Gordon S. Wood, *Empire of Liberty: A History of the Early Republic, 1789-1815,* 2009

**The patterns described in the excerpt most directly signal which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Regional interests outweighed national concerns for many political leaders’ positions on economic policy
American foreign policy became more isolationist as the United States refused to maintain peacetime alliances
Labor unions were developed as a way to protect workers from unsafe business practices
Disputes over the expansion of slavery to newly acquired territories caused the end of the Whig party

assessment_items.question_content:
> “In terms of international power politics, the Monroe Doctrine represented the moment when the United States felt strong enough to assert a ‘sphere of influence’ that other powers must respect. In terms of national psychology, the Monroe Doctrine marked the moment when Americans no longer faced eastward across the Atlantic and turned to face westward across the continent. The changed orientation was reflected in domestic political alignments.” 

>-Source: Daniel Walker Howe, *What Hath God Wrought: The Transformation of America, 1815-1848,* 2007

**Which of the following historical events best illustrates the general argument in the excerpt about how Americans “turned to face westward across the continent”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Mexican-American War
The Second Great Awakening
The Nullification Crisis
The Embargo Act of 1807

assessment_items.question_content:
> “In terms of international power politics, the Monroe Doctrine represented the moment when the United States felt strong enough to assert a ‘sphere of influence’ that other powers must respect. In terms of national psychology, the Monroe Doctrine marked the moment when Americans no longer faced eastward across the Atlantic and turned to face westward across the continent. The changed orientation was reflected in domestic political alignments.” 

>-Source: Daniel Walker Howe, *What Hath God Wrought: The Transformation of America, 1815-1848,* 2007

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the early nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The establishment of the United States as a major world power in the Western Hemisphere
The rise in a nativist movement targeting German and Irish immigrants
An increase in support for women’s right to vote
An improvement in technology, leading to more innovation

assessment_items.question_content:
> “In terms of international power politics, the Monroe Doctrine represented the moment when the United States felt strong enough to assert a ‘sphere of influence’ that other powers must respect. In terms of national psychology, the Monroe Doctrine marked the moment when Americans no longer faced eastward across the Atlantic and turned to face westward across the continent. The changed orientation was reflected in domestic political alignments.” 

>-Source: Daniel Walker Howe, *What Hath God Wrought: The Transformation of America, 1815-1848,* 2007

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that the Monroe Doctrine had on the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States protected countries in the Western Hemisphere from further European intervention
The United States acquired Texas along with territory in present-day California
The United States strengthened an alliance with Britain
The United States decreased funds to their peace-time military

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/199cee9eacd4c579af01232e869910b0.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monroe_doctrine.jpg) 1912

**The image most directly illustrates a United States foreign policy that emphasized which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The dominance of the United States as the major power in the Western Hemisphere
The authority of the United States to interfere in European wars
The expansion of American territorial holdings into Mexico and Guatemala
The efforts of the United States to spread democracy in Latin America to stop the influence of Communism

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/199cee9eacd4c579af01232e869910b0.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monroe_doctrine.jpg) 1912

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon about the Monroe Doctrine?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: It declared that the Western Hemisphere was off-limits to European intervention.
It protected Latin America from United States expansion.
It was incredibly ineffective at protecting South America from involvement in European wars.
It  crafted the belief that the United States had the God-given right to expand westward.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/199cee9eacd4c579af01232e869910b0.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monroe_doctrine.jpg) 1912

**The policy suggested in the image was most directly a response to which of the following early nineteenth century historical development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The successful independence movements by Latin American countries
The continued conflicts between Britain and France in the Napoleonic Wars
The expansion of Mexico into present-day California, Utah, and New Mexico
The armed rebellion of Texas against the government of Mexico
